mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment, which boosts consumer income and spending, driving corporate earnings and equity prices higher over the next month.
failure_condition: The rolling 2-year Rank IC of the 1-month change in PAYEMS with 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03, or positive PAYEMS changes are followed by negative returns in more than 50% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI year-over-year inflation exceeds 3%, the Federal Reserve is likely to tighten monetary policy, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In periods where CPI YoY exceeds 3%, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is greater than 50% over a rolling 3-year window, or the average return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in both employment (PAYEMS) and industrial production (INDPRO) over three months signals broad-based economic expansion, boosting confidence and capital spending, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and INDPRO show positive 3-month percentage change
failure_condition: When both proxies rise over 3 months, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of such episodes over a 5-year rolling window, or the joint signal does not produce a mean return significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4%, the labor market is considered overheated, leading to wage inflation and margin compression that eventually depress corporate profits and equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: In periods with UNRATE below 4%, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns is greater than 40% over a rolling 3-year window, or the mean return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect robust consumer demand, which directly boosts corporate revenues and earnings, leading to positive short-term equity returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: The rolling 2-year Rank IC of the month-over-month change in RSAFS with 5-day forward returns remains below 0.02, or positive RSAFS changes are followed by negative returns in more than 45% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) of more than 10% signals a sudden weakening in the housing market, which typically precipitates broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: After HOUST drops more than 10% m/m, the proportion of positive 20-day SPX returns exceeds 50% over any 5-year rolling window, or the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) are above their 6-month moving average and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is below 2%, the economy is in a low-rate, high-demand environment that stimulates investment and spending, producing positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 50% of such months over a 5-year rolling window, or the mean return is not statistically positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeds 3% annualized, it signals above-potential growth that may trigger overheating concerns and anticipatory monetary tightening, leading to negative equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When GDP growth exceeds 3%, the proportion of positive 60-day SPX returns is greater than 40% over any 10-year rolling window, or the mean return is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.10).
